title: >-
  Popular pet food recalled over possible sharp metal and plastic contamination
  - Fox Business
date: '2026-07-08'
month: 2026-07
source: Google News
sourceUrl: >-
  https://news.google.?oc=5
location: United States
species:
  - dogs

  - metal

severity: high
status: confirmed
type: recall
summary: >-
  Mars Petcare US issued a voluntary recall on July 2 for two lots of Pedigree
  High Protein Chopped Chicken & Duck Flavor dog food in 13.2-ounce cans. The
  lot codes 613C3KKCFC and 613C1KKCFC were sent to a third-party vendor for
  destruction because they did not meet safety and quality standards, but they
  were later found to have been fraudulently diverted and sold into the U.S.
  market. The cans may contain sharp metal and plastic, which could cause
  choking, lacerations, or gastrointestinal blockages in dogs. No illnesses or
  injuries have been reported. Consumers who purchased the affected product
  should not feed it to their pets and should contact Pedigree for a
  replacement.
relatedSlugs:
  pesticides:
    - metaldehyde
    - snail-bait

clusterId: 1094760ad6ca45b4
---
Mars Petcare US issued a voluntary recall on July 2 for two lots of Pedigree High Protein Chopped Chicken & Duck Flavor dog food in 13.2-ounce cans. The lot codes 613C3KKCFC and 613C1KKCFC were sent to a third-party vendor for destruction because they did not meet safety and quality standards, but they were later found to have been fraudulently diverted and sold into the U.S. market. The cans may contain sharp metal and plastic, which could cause choking, lacerations, or gastrointestinal blockages in dogs. No illnesses or injuries have been reported. Consumers who purchased the affected product should not feed it to their pets and should contact Pedigree for a replacement.

[Read the full report on Google News →](https://news.google.?oc=5)
